description_long: This house features a traditional Japanese architectural style with a rectangular layout and a prominent, dark-tiled gable roof. The exterior walls are composed of light-colored panels framed with dark wooden beams, evoking the look of shoji screens. The structure includes a covered veranda supported by slender columns, extending along the front and partially wrapping around the sides. The open courtyard area is visible from above, suggesting an L-shaped floor plan with an enclosed garden or patio space. The entryway is marked by a small overhang and steps, providing a sheltered approach. Minimalist in ornamentation, the house emphasizes clean lines, symmetry, and a harmonious blend with its surroundings.
description: A traditional Japanese-style house with a dark gable roof, light panel walls, and a covered veranda supported by columns. The layout is L-shaped with an open courtyard and a sheltered entryway.
description_view_0: This view highlights the L-shaped layout, the dark gable roof, and the enclosed courtyard area.
description_view_1: The front view shows the covered veranda with supporting columns and the main entryway under the roof overhang.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the length of the roof and the arrangement of the light panel walls.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the symmetry of the wall panels and the open courtyard space within the L-shaped structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with a small overhang, steps, and a covered veranda supported by columns
